description: Technical safeguard rules derived from HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR Part 164)

rules.description: PHI must not appear in log statements. Logging PHI creates unauthorized disclosure risk and violates audit control requirements.
rules.developer_message: Remove PHI from log statements. Use anonymized identifiers or patient IDs instead. See: https://your-org.com/security/phi-logging-policy

rules.description: PHI must not appear in exception or error messages. Error messages containing PHI may be exposed in stack traces, monitoring tools, or bug reports.
rules.developer_message: Remove PHI from exception messages. Raise generic errors and log details separately with proper access controls.

rules.description: PHI must not be hardcoded in source code. Hardcoded PHI is exposed to anyone with repository access and cannot be rotated.
rules.developer_message: Remove hardcoded PHI. Use environment variables, secrets managers (AWS Secrets Manager, HashiCorp Vault), or encrypted configuration files.

rules.description: PHI written to files or storage must be encrypted. Writing PHI in plaintext violates the encryption and decryption safeguard requirement.
rules.developer_message: Encrypt PHI before writing to disk. Use AES-256 or a secrets manager. Never write raw PHI to temp files or local storage.

rules.description: PHI must not be transmitted over unencrypted HTTP. All PHI in transit must use TLS (HTTPS).
rules.developer_message: Replace http:// with https:// for all endpoints that handle PHI. Enforce TLS 1.2 or higher.

rules.description: Weak hashing algorithms (MD5, SHA1) must not be used for PHI. These algorithms are cryptographically broken and do not meet HIPAA encryption standards.
rules.developer_message: Replace MD5/SHA1 with SHA-256 or stronger for any operation involving PHI. Use bcrypt or Argon2 for password hashing.

rules.description: PHI must not appear in URL query parameters. Query parameters are logged by web servers, proxies, and browsers, creating unauthorized disclosure risk.
rules.developer_message: Move PHI out of URL query strings. Use POST request bodies with encrypted payloads instead. Never include SSN, MRN, or patient identifiers in URLs.

rules.description: PHI must not appear in code comments. Comments are stored in source control and visible to all repository contributors.
rules.developer_message: Remove PHI from comments. Use synthetic or anonymized identifiers in code examples and documentation.

rules.description: SQL queries involving PHI must use parameterized queries. String formatting or concatenation in SQL queries creates SQL injection risk, enabling unauthorized PHI access.
rules.developer_message: Use parameterized queries or ORM methods for all database operations involving PHI. Never use string formatting (%s, f-strings, .format()) to build SQL queries.

rules.description: PHI must not be stored in browser cookies or client-side storage. Client-side storage is accessible to JavaScript and third-party scripts.
rules.developer_message: Do not store PHI in cookies, localStorage, or sessionStorage. Use server-side session management with encrypted, HttpOnly, Secure cookies for session tokens only.
